items.title: Faster LTS and free JDK with Java 17 - Inside Java Newscast #12
items.summary: Java 17 comes with more than just new features. A faster LTS cadence and free Oracle JDK make this the best-supported modern release ever.

items.title: Better Random Number Generation in Java 17
items.summary: Java 17 expands the API for random number generation to make it more usable, extensible, and robust with RandomGenerator and RandomGeneratorFactory at its core.

items.title: The State of Project Valhalla with Brian Goetz
items.summary: Conversation with Project Valhalla lead Brian Goetz about Java's original sin, unifying the type system, expanding generics, current work, the project timeline, and more.

items.title: Fast and Secure Inter-process Communication on JDK 16 - Inside Java Newscast #11
items.summary: JDK 16's socket channel / server-socket channel API can use Unix domain sockets for faster and more secure inter-process communication on the same host - also: JDK 17 final release candidate and Oracle Developer Live

items.title: Random Numbers and JDK Flight Recorder - Inside Java Newscast #9
items.summary: The new API for random number generation in Java 17 - why it needed to change and how the new API is more usable, extensible, and robust - and how to get started with JDK Flight Recorder, particularly on Java 16.

items.title: Incremental Evolution, Pattern Switches vs Visitor Pattern, and Wayland Support - Inside Java Newscast #8
items.summary: How the six-month release cadence enabled a more incremental evolution of the Java platform and how pattern switches and sealed classes are an alternative to the visitor pattern. Also, maybe Wayland support for Java.

items.title: Visitor Pattern Considered Pointless - Use Pattern Switches Instead
items.summary: In modern Java, the visitor pattern is no longer needed. Using sealed types and switches with pattern matching achieves the same goals with less code and less complexity.

items.title: Handling null and Upgrading Past Java 8 - Inside Java Newscast #7
items.summary: Dealing with null is never fun, so in this newscast, I explain how to best handle it, what tools can do, and how recent and upcoming language changes help dealing with it. I'll also look at a recent blog post about how Netflix upgraded from Java 8 to 16.

items.title: The State of Project Panama with Maurizio Cimadamore
items.summary: Conversation with Project Panama lead Maurizio Cimadamore about the project's core mission, the split into foreign-memory access and foreign linker APIs, jextract, performance, interaction with Project Valhalla, the timeline, and more.

items.title: Java Modules in Real Life
items.summary: Advice on why, when, when not, and how to use Java modules in real life for your projects

items.title: The State of Project Loom with Ron Pressler
items.summary: Conversation with Project Loom lead Ron Pressler about the project's core mission, challenges like interaction with debuggers and garbage collectors, the timeline for the next steps, compatibility and more.

items.title: Java 17: Features and Development - Inside Java Newscast #6
items.summary: Java 17, the next long-term support release, enters feature freeze and the release preparations begin today (June 10th). A good time to take a closer look at the list of JEPs as well as the development process.

items.title: Pattern Matching in Switches and Security Manager Deprecation - Inside Java Newscast #5
items.summary: JEP 406, which is a candidate for Java 17, introduced pattern matching in switch statements and expressions, introduces guarded patterns, and improves null handling. Then we need to talk about JEP 411's deprecation of the security manager.

items.title: 26 Hours of Java
items.summary: On May 29th we'll throw a late birthday party for Java, which turned 26 a few days before. With a 26-hour live stream relay race! 🥳

items.title: 5 Secret Java API Tricks and Record Semantics - Inside Java Newscast #4
items.summary: Five nifty Java API features that you need to know (and many more in the linked thread) and a quick explanation why Java records are not about reducing boilerplate.

items.title: Why Java's Records Are Better* Than Lombok's @Data and Kotlin's Data Classes
items.summary: While all three remove boilerplate, the similarities don't go much further. Records have stronger semantics with important downstream benefits, which makes them better*. (* not always; depends on circumstances; excuse the clickbait)

items.title: Why Don't They Just... ?! The Deliberations Behind Evolving Java
items.summary: There are many nifty features that Java could have but doesn't. Somewhat surprisingly, there are reasons for that and in this talk I'll discuss those for a few concrete cases as well as the deliberations behind such decisions.

items.title: JUnit Pioneer - An Exploratory Mission to Jupiter And Beyond
items.summary: From growing a community on Twitch to strong documentation, from squashing commits to one-click releases - this presentation covers JUnit Pioneer in all detail

items.title: Quicker Java and JDK 16 compatibility - Inside Java Newscast #3
items.summary: A walk through language features, APIs, and JDK capabilities that make Java quicker to use with less ceremony and more immediate results. Also, a rundown of some of the projects with all tests green on JDK 16.

items.title: Java Generics III - Wildcards
items.summary: Second part of a short series on Java Generics - this one explains generics. ? extends Number - that kinda thing.

items.title: Java Generics II - Bounded Type Parameters
items.summary: Second part of a short series on Java generics - this one explains bounded type parameters. T extends Number - that kinda thing.

items.title: Vector API, Record Serialization, And Java 17 Release Schedule - Inside Java Newscast #2
items.summary: Short introduction to the Vector API (incubating in JDK 16) and an update on serializing records. Also, a quick mention of JEP 356 in JDK 17 and the proposed release schedule.

items.title: Java 16 Rundown, First Of Java 17 - Inside Java Newscast #1
items.summary: Java 16 got released, so I go over most of the additions like records, Stream APIs, Unix Domain Socket support, and much more. Then there's a first glimpse at Java 17.

items.title: To Jupiter And Beyond - On An Exploratory Mission With JUnit Pioneer
items.summary: JUnit Pioneer gathers JUnit 5 extensions. This talk discusses the technical aspects, but also the mission, dev practices, automatic releases, and what Twitch has to do with all of this.

items.title: Definitive Guide To Java 16
items.summary: A detailed guide to Java 16: records, type patterns, sealed classes; Stream and HTTP/2 additions, Unix domain socket support; Project Panama previews, packaging tool, performance improvements, and more

items.title: Patterns, switch, and Valhalla's Primitive Classes - JDK News #2
items.summary: Project Amber brings new patterns and puts them into switch while Project Valhalla takes off and proposes introducing primitive classes

items.title: Code-First Unix Domain Socket Tutorial
items.summary: Java's socket channel / server-socket channel API can use Unix domain sockets for faster and more secure inter-process communication on the same host

items.title: Type Pattern Matching with instanceof
items.summary: Type patters with instanceof are Java's first step towards pattern matching. Motivation, syntax, details, and future applications - here's all you need to know.

items.title: Pattern Matching in Java
items.summary: Java takes its first steps towards pattern matching but the topic is much larger than instanceof . Goals, terminology, flow scoping - these apply to all kinds of patterns.

items.title: Java's Quirks and Wrong (?) Defaults with Brian Goetz
items.summary: Mutability, nullability, serialization, primitives - Nicolai Parlog discusses with Java language architect Brian Goetz why Java is the way it is.

items.title: Pattern Matching Quartet - JDK News #1
items.summary: A summary of four recent discussions about pattern matching on the Project Amber mailing lists

items.title: Sorting A React App Into Java's Folder Structure
items.summary: How to use react-app-rewired to sort a React app into a Java folder structure with package.json at the root, and sources in src/{main|test}/js

items.title: Java 2077
items.summary: The year is 2077, the Java version is 128. It's not LTS. Here's how Loom, Valhalla, Panama, Leyden, Amber, and Jigsaw pushed humanity to the brink. And how you can save us.

items.title: Broken Stream::group with Java 16's mapMulti
items.summary: Java 16 adds a new method mapMulti to Stream and it can be abused to simulate a reverse- flatMap aka group operation (with shortcomings).

items.title: Faster flatMaps with Stream::mapMulti in Java 16
items.summary: Java 16 adds a new method mapMulti to Stream . It fills the same role as flatMap , but is more imperative - and faster.

items.title: Java Modules Cheat Sheet
items.summary: A cheat sheet for building and running Java modules from the command line with javac , jar , and java

items.title: JUnit Pioneer 1.0
items.summary: Yesterday we released JUnit Pioneer 1.0 🥳 - here's a quick rundown of its features

items.title: Java Generics I - The Basics
items.summary: First part of a short series on Java generics - this one explains the basics

items.title: 25 Hours of Java
items.summary: On May 23rd 2020, Java turns 25 🥳 and what better way to celebrate its birthday than with a 25-hour live stream? (On Twitch: https://twitch.tv/nipafx)

items.title: Modularity with Oliver Drotbohm
items.summary: Oliver and I discuss modularity in Java with a focus on the Java module system

items.title: Java After Seventeen
items.summary: A live-coding talk during which I update a Java 11/17 code base to Java 21, making good use of new language features, additional and improved APIs, and JVM capabilities

items.title: How We Upgraded From Java 8 And Why You Can (And Should) Do It Too
items.summary: My two minutes of fame during the Oracle Code One 2019 keynote

items.title: Definitive Guide To Java 13
items.summary: A detailed guide to Java 13: text blocks, switch expressions with yield, ZGC, dynamic AppCDS archives

items.title: Improve Launch Times On Java 13 With Application Class-Data Sharing
items.summary: On Java 10+, you can use application class-data sharing to reduce launch times, response time outliers, and memory footprint. By archiving class data with -Xshare:dump and loading it with -Xshare:on, the JVM's class loading workload can be reduced considerably.

items.title: Definitive Guide To Switch Expressions In Java 13
items.summary: Java 13 finalized switch expressions. Together with a new lambda-style arrow syntax, this makes switch more expressive and less error-prone.

items.title: The JPMS Maturity Model
items.summary: Java's module system requires consistent support by libraries, frameworks, and tools. This maturity model classifies a project's support for the JPMS.

items.title: Evolving Java With ––enable–preview aka Preview Language Features
items.summary: Use --enable-preview (plus --source or --release during compilation) to experiment with Java's preview features

items.title: Definitive Guide To Text Blocks In Java 13
items.summary: Java 13 introduces text blocks: string literals that span multiple lines. Learn about syntax, indentation, escape sequences, and formatting.

items.title: Best Practices Considered Harmful
items.summary: A lightning talk about how best practices promote a one-size-fits all mentality that harms our ability to create solutions that are tailored to the problems at hand

items.title: Immutable Collections In Java - Not Now, Not Ever
items.summary: The JDK contains immutable collections, but no type ImmutableCollection . Here's why that's so and why it won't change.

items.title: The Java Module System with Sander Mak
items.summary: At J-Fall 2018 I talked to Sander Mak, modularity expert at Luminis, about the Java module system (J_MS), its adoption, how it compares to OSGi, and more.

items.title: Caliz III: Hashing scripts and background compilation
items.summary: Letting Caliz store native images and only create them for a given Java "script" (single source file) if needed

items.title: Jakarta EE, javax, And A Week Of Turmoil
items.summary: During a week of turmoil, many people have written about Jakarta EE and javax. This post summarizes the community's opinions and gives you plenty of links.

items.title: Caliz II: Wrapping Graal AOT
items.summary: Extending Caliz to create native images of Java "scripts" (single source files) with with Graal

items.title: Caliz I: Wrapping JVM 11 and learning about Graal AOT
items.summary: First steps toward an acceptable scripting experience with single-source-file execution and Graal native images

items.title: Definitive Guide To Java 12
items.summary: Detailed Java 12 guide: migration, versions; switch expressions, teeing collectors, indenting/transforming strings (and more); default CDS, Shenandoah, G1.

items.title: Teeing Collector in Java 12
items.summary: The teeing collector, available since Java 12 as Collectors::teeing, forwards its input to two other collectors before merging their results with a function.

items.title: Java 12 Experiments (Live Stream)
items.summary: In my first live stream ever (yay!), we explored Java 12's API improvements

items.title: Utilities, Singletons and Dependency Injection - Effective Java, Items 3-5
items.summary: Mildly surprising (to me), it makes sense to discuss these three patters in one video - so here it goes

items.title: Eleven Hidden Gems In Java 11
items.summary: Eleven small but shiny additions in Java 11 to classes like String , Path , Files , Collection , Optional , and others that make coding a little more elegant.
